title: Crataegus × silicensis
taxonomy: Crataegus × silicensis is a hawthorn hybrid, a cross between two different species of the genus Crataegus. It is a member of the Rosaceae family, a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The Rosaceae family includes many common and ornamental species, such as roses, pears, and apples. Crataegus × silicensis is native to the Czech Republic and was first cultivated in the 20th century. This hybrid hawthorn is valued for its attractive flowers and tolerance to various environmental conditions.
uses: Crataegus × silicensis is a hybrid hawthorn species, and its medicinal uses have not been extensively documented. However, hawthorn species in general have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. They are believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and cardiovascular benefits. The flowers, leaves, and fruits of hawthorn plants are often used in teas, tinctures, and extracts. These preparations are thought to help lower blood pressure, improve heart health, and reduce symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
benefits: Crataegus × silicensis is known for its potential health benefits, particularly in relation to cardiovascular health. The plant contains flavonoids and other compounds that may help to lower cholesterol levels and reduce inflammation. Additionally, it may also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Some studies suggest that extracts from Crataegus × silicensis may have a positive effect on blood pressure and lipid profiles. Further research is needed to fully understand the extent of its health benefits.

intro: Crataegus × sourbaya is an ornamental hawthorn tree hybrid, resulting from the cross between Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. The leaves and fruits of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to support cardiovascular health, with potential benefits including lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The bioactive constituents of Crataegus × sourbaya include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and oligomeric proanthocyanidin complexes, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Crataegus × sourbaya include extracts, tinctures, and teas, which are often used to promote heart health and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia.
